new_classes.label_en: PDO wine (other, red and rosé), in containers ≤ 2 l (CN 2204 21 74)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.), anderer, rot und rosé, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (KN 2204 21 74)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), other than Tokaj and other enumerated PDO wines covered by 2204 21 23–2204 21 68, red and rosé, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, falling under CN subheading 2204 21 74.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for the range 2204 21 11–2204 21 78 establishes that all these codes cover wines with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as per additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22. The key discriminating criteria captured are: (1) PDO wine production process (producedBy PDOWineProductionProcess22042100), (2) small container ≤ 2 l (hasPart some SmallContainer), and (3) exclusion of sibling PDO wine codes already modelled (Tokaj 22042123, other PDO 22042168). However, the legal text for CN 2204 21 74 specifically refers to red and rosé wines (colour criterion), and the sub-range distinction within PDO wines (e.g. by actual alcoholic strength and wine colour) that places a wine in 22042174 vs. adjacent codes (22042171 white; 22042175–22042178 higher strength red/rosé) cannot be fully captured because: (a) there is no existing 'RedOrRoséWineQuality' class or 'WineColour' quality in the TBox, and (b) the alcoholic strength thresholds subdividing PDO still wines in containers ≤ 2 l (e.g. ≤ 15% vol vs. > 15% vol) are not yet modelled. A WineColourRedRoséQuality class would be needed to fully discriminate this code from white PDO wine codes. Coverage is therefore partial — the PDO + small container + negative sibling constraints are captured but the red/rosé colour and strength-band criteria are missing.
